item.title: Global equity fund inflows surge on Middle East ceasefire boost
item.description: Global equity fund inflows nearly doubled in the week through April 8 as a two-week Middle East ceasefire raised hopes вҖӢof a resumption of shipments through the Strait of вҖҢHormuz.

item.title: The unconventional logic behind SpaceX's $1.75 trillion price tag
item.description: Wall Street is reaching for some unusual yardsticks to price Elon Musk's SpaceX.

item.title: Morning Bid: Back from the brink?
item.description: Everything Mike Dolan and the ROI team are excited to read, watch and listen to over the weekend.

item.title: Big Tech puts financial heft behind next-gen nuclear power as AI demand surges
item.description: Big Tech is reshaping the funding landscape for new nuclear technologies as it seeks to bolster electricity supply for вҖӢpower-hungry AI data centers, inking deals that offer nuclear companies both funding and a clearer path to making money.

item.title: Warburg Pincus, Munich Re arm team up to invest in Europe's defence sector
item.description: U.S. private equity firm Warburg Pincus has launched an investment platform focused вҖӢon Europe's defence and security вҖҢsector, with MEAG, the asset manager of reinsurer Munich Re , joining as an early investor.

item.title: US trade court weighs legality of Trump 10% global tariff
item.description: A U.S. trade court on Friday will consider the legality of a 10% global import tax imposed by the Trump administration, which вҖӢseveral states and small businesses say sidesteps a U.S. Supreme Court ruling вҖҢthat invalidated most of Trump's previous tariffs.

item.title: US utility stocks are off to their best start since 2019. Can they keep it up?
item.description: U.S. utility stocks marked their вҖӢstrongest start to the year since 2019, benefiting from an investor retreat from riskier assets during the Iran вҖҢwar and strong electricity demand from firms building out artificial intelligence infrastructure.

item.title: Shippers weigh unusual routes as high air cargo rates, ocean gridlock persist
item.description: Some shippers caught between high jet fuel prices and congestion in vital Middle East waterways are now looking for transport routes вҖҢin unexpected places, with a recent ceasefire between Iran and the United States unlikely to offer quick relief.

item.title: Crypto giant Kraken's Fed payment account sparks concerns about risks
item.description: Crypto giant Kraken's landmark Federal Reserve master account comes with restrictions aimed at mitigating risks, but it - and others likely to follow in its wake - вҖӢcould still create vulnerabilities for the U.S. financial system.

item.title: Insight: How the AI boom derailed cleanвҖ‘air efforts in one of America's most polluted cities
item.description: Barbara Johnson has been fighting coal pollution for decades in her mostly Black neighborhood of North St. Louis as an organizer with Metropolitan Congregations United вҖ“ one of many activist groups campaigning for cleaner air in a city that has some of the countryвҖҷs dirtiest.

item.title: US stock futures subdued ahead of inflation data, Mideast in focus
item.description: U.S. stock index futures were вҖӢlargely muted on Friday as investors stuck to the sidelines ahead of a crucial вҖҢinflation print, while assessing the fragility of the Middle East truce.

item.title: EU and US near critical minerals deal to combat Chinese control, Bloomberg News reports
item.description: The European Union and Washington are вҖӢclosing in on an agreement to coordinate вҖҢon producing and securing critical minerals, Bloomberg News reported on Friday.

item.title: Apple leads global smartphone shipments in first quarter, Counterpoint says
item.description: iPhone-maker Apple вҖӢled smartphone вҖҢshipments in the вҖӢfirst quarter, вҖӢgrowing 5% year-on-year, вҒ even вҖӢas overall вҖӢglobal shipments remained under вҖӢpressure вҖӢdue to a вҖҢshortage вҒ of memory components and weak вҖӢconsumer вҖӢsentiment, вҒ Counterpoint Research вҖӢsaid вҖӢon вҒ Friday.

item.title: Polls in Hungary and Peru next in key election year for world markets
item.description: Voters in Hungary could deliver a boon to bond investors, while Peru's weekend polls will help determine the next step for its economy, the latest in a string of key elections worldwide this year.

item.title: Daimler Truck Q1 unit sales decline on weak North America demand
item.description: Daimler вҖҢTruck , one of the world's largest truckmakers, on Friday вҖӢreported a 9% вҖӢdecline in first-quarter group unit sales, weighed вҒ down by a вҖӢsharp slowdown in North America.

item.title: SpaceX begins installing equipment at Texas facility, eyes year-end production, sources say
item.description: SpaceX has begun installing equipment at its advanced chip packaging facility in Bastrop, Texas, as the satellite and rocket вҖӢcompany aims to begin production there by the end вҖӢof this year, two sources familiar with the matter вҒ told Reuters.

item.title: Canada's Cohere, Germany's Aleph Alpha in merger talks, Handelsblatt reports
item.description: Artificial intelligence companies Cohere of Canada and Aleph вҖӢAlpha of Germany are in talks to вҖҢmerge and have Berlin's support for a potential deal, newspaper Handelsblatt reported late on Thursday.

item.title: Hyundai to recall over 294,000 US vehicles over seat belt anchor defect
item.description: Hyundai Motor вҖӢAmerica is recalling 294,128 vehicles вҖҢin the U.S. due to a defect that could вҖӢcause seat belt вҖӢanchors to detach, the U.S. вҒ National Highway Traffic Safety вҖӢAdministration said on Friday.

item.title: Porsche deliveries tumble further in first quarter
item.description: Porsche AG's deliveries slumped further in the first three months of 2026, with sharp вҖӢdeclines in key markets China and the вҖҢUnited States, as loses its shine across.

item.title: Stefano Gabbana has stepped down as Dolce & Gabbana chairman, filing shows
item.description: Stefano Gabbana, co-founder вҖӢof Italian luxury вҖҢfashion house Dolce & Gabbana, stepped down вҖӢas chairman вҖӢlast January, according to вҒ a company вҖӢfiling with the вҖӢlocal chamber of commerce.

item.title: TSMC's Q1 revenue jumps 35% y/y, beats market forecasts
item.description: The world's largest contract chipmaker, TSMC, reported first-quarter вҖӢrevenue of T$1.134 trillion ($35.71 billion) on Friday, rising вҖҢ35% on the year to beat market forecasts on surging interest in artificial intelligence (AI) applications.

item.title: Sodexo cuts 2026 targets as new CEO gears to tackle long-standing issues
item.description: French food caterer Sodexo slashed its annual sales and profitability targets on Friday, citing execution challenges and its management's review of contracts and assets, which sent its shares falling 13%.

item.title: Morning Bid: 'That is not the agreement we have!'
item.description: A look at the day ahead in European and global markets from Gregor Stuart Hunter

item.title: US consumer inflation expected to have surged in March amid Iran war
item.description: U.S. consumer prices likely recorded their biggest increase in nearly four years in March as the war with Iran boosted oil prices and the pass-through from tariffs persisted, which would further diminish hopes for an interest rate cut this вҖӢyear.

item.title: Jury orders Abbott to pay $53 million in preterm infant formula trial, media report says
item.description: A jury in Chicago on Thursday said Abbott Laboratories must pay $53 million in compensatory damages to a group of families that had accused the company of failing to warn that its formula for premature infants can cause a potentially deadly bowel disease, according to the Chicago Tribune.

item.title: China's factories jolt back to inflation on Iran war price shock
item.description: China's factory-gate prices rose for вҖӢthe first time in 3-1/2 years in March, official data showed, in an вҖҢearly sign that the Middle East conflict is feeding cost pressures into the worldвҖҷs second-largest economy.

item.title: Japan rules out major domestic risks from private credit for now
item.description: Japanese Finance Minister Satsuki Katayama said on Friday private credit does not вҖӢpose a major issue domestically at present but noted вҖҢrisks linked to the $2 trillion industry could be discussed at next week's G7 finance meeting.

item.title: Chinese startup ShengShu raises $293 million to advance artificial general intelligence
item.description: Chinese artificial intelligence startup ShengShu Technology has raised 2 billion yuan ($292.59 million) in a funding round led by Alibaba Cloud, вҖӢthe company said on Friday, as competition intensifies in вҖҢChina's AI sector.

item.title: Dollar set for weekly drop ahead of US-Iran peace talks
item.description: The dollar on Friday was heading for its largest weekly drop since January as other currencies gained on optimism that a ceasefire in the Gulf will hold and oil shipping will resume.

item.title: Stocks shaky as Israeli attacks on Lebanon tests Iran ceasefire
item.description: Asian stocks ticked up early on Friday but gains вҖӢwere capped as traders questioned the durability of this week's U.S.-Iran ceasefire and remained wary of fragile hopes for вҖҢIsrael-Lebanon peace talks.
